title: Collabium vesicatum
taxonomy: Collabium vesicatum is classified as a genus of orchid. It belongs to the family Orchidaceae. This orchid is native to tropical Asia. Further classification places Collabium vesicatum within the subfamily Epidendroideae. The genus Collabium is a small group of orchid species.

intro: Collabium vesicatum, also known as Collabium, is a plant species that belongs to the orchid family (Orchidaceae). It has been traditionally used in herbal medicine for its various health benefits, including anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which can help to alleviate symptoms of arthritis, improve immune function, and protect against oxidative stress. The bioactive constituents of Collabium vesicatum include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and saponins, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. Collabium is commonly used to make teas, infusions, and capsules, as well as being incorporated into traditional herbal remedies, such as tinctures and extracts.
